requirements.description: The application must be a command-line tool written in Python that prompts the user to input a number n.
The tool must calculate the factorial of the input number n using a recursive function.
The tool must include basic error handling to detect and handle negative numbers, informing the user appropriately.
The tool must include basic error handling to detect and handle non-integer inputs, informing the user appropriately.
The tool must format the output clearly and make it user-friendly, such as displaying the result in a readable message.

assumptions: The user has Python installed and can run the script in a standard command-line environment.
The input is provided via standard input (e.g., keyboard).
Factorial is only defined for non-negative integers, and the tool will not handle extremely large numbers that cause recursion depth issues.
success_criteria: The tool correctly prompts for input and computes the factorial of a valid non-negative integer using recursion.
The tool handles invalid inputs (negative numbers or non-integers) by displaying an appropriate error message without crashing.
The output is clearly formatted, making it easy for the user to understand the result or error.
